title: Require at least 1 classical oversold signal (RSI or BelowBB) for any qualifying score — regime-only scores do not trigger entry
analysis: The journal shows a persistent pattern of scores reaching the 2-3 threshold exclusively via regime/math signals with zero RSI or Bollinger Band confirmation. Across 20+ XRP/USDT sessions on May 7-8, every qualifying score (2/6) was composed of BelowMA20>1% + ADX<25 only — RSI averaged 47-49 (neutral), pct_b averaged 0.25-0.36 (mid-band). META sessions on May 6-7 scored 4 via RSI<45 + BelowMA20>1% + ADX<25 + PositiveSentiment — but RSI<45 is not in the formal scoring rubric (RSI<40 is the threshold), meaning the effective formal score was 3 with only 1 marginal oversold signal. The May 7 14:34 entry explicitly notes: 'RSI<45 (mild oversold)' fired but 'RSI<40 (+1pt) — NOT FIRED — RSI is 43.2 (just above 40)' — a signal the bot itself acknowledges doesn't formally qualify. On May 8-9, LTC/LINK scored 4 via ADX<25 + Wavelet + Hurst + Levy — all regime math signals with RSI 65-71. Zero classical oversold signals in any of these qualifying setups.
rationale: Regime signals (ADX<20/25, Hurst, Levy, Wavelet) confirm the market ENVIRONMENT is suitable for mean reversion. They say nothing about whether THIS asset at THIS moment is at a mean reversion entry point. The RSI and BelowBB signals are the only ones that directly measure price dislocation from the mean. Requiring at least 1 of {RSI<40, BelowBB, BelowVWAP>1%} as a mandatory component of any qualifying score ensures the score reflects both environment AND dislocation — the two necessary ingredients for a mean reversion trade.

insight.why_now: Every qualifying-score SKIP in the journal (XRP/USDT May 7-8, META May 6-7, LTC/LINK May 8-9) was skipped via qualitative override because the bot recognized RSI was neutral or overbought despite the score qualifying. This represents a systematic gap between the scoring rule and the trading intent. The rule currently allows qualification without any evidence of oversold conditions.
insight.purpose: Add a minimum composition requirement: the qualifying score of 3+ must include at least 1 point from {RSI<32 (+2pts), RSI<40 (+1pt), BelowBB (+1pt), BelowVWAP>1% (+1pt)}. Pure regime scores (ADX + MA deviation + sentiment) cannot trigger entry alone. This aligns the mechanical rule with the consistent qualitative reasoning observed in 25+ journal entries.
insight.expected_effect: Eliminates all observed false-positive qualifying scores. META at RSI 43-47 would still qualify (RSI<40 is borderline — the 14:34 entry shows RSI 43.2 which would now require a precise check). LTC/LINK at RSI 65-71 would be blocked. XRP at RSI 47-49 with no BB breach would be blocked. Estimated reduction in false-positive qualifying sessions: ~85% of observed cases. Trade frequency reduction: significant for crypto, minimal for equity setups.
insight.risks: In a quiet ranging market where RSI sits at 38-42 and price is slightly below the mean but not breaching the lower BB, the required 1-point oversold signal (RSI<40) would fire at RSI<40 and allow the entry. This is correct behavior. Risk is that in some instruments, RSI rarely reaches 40 during mild pullbacks — the bot would skip valid but shallow mean reversion setups. Mitigated by keeping RSI<40 (not just RSI<32) as sufficient.

current_rule:
## SIGNAL SCORING (need 3+ to enter)
- RSI < 32: +2 points (strong oversold)
- RSI < 40: +1 point (mild oversold)
- Price below lower Bollinger Band: +1 point
- Price below VWAP by > 1%: +1 point
- Positive news sentiment score: +1 point
- ADX < 20 (ranging market): +1 point (regime confirmation)
proposed_rule:
## SIGNAL SCORING (need 3+ to enter)
- RSI < 32: +2 points (strong oversold)
- RSI < 40: +1 point (mild oversold)
- Price below lower Bollinger Band: +1 point
- Price below VWAP by > 1%: +1 point
- Positive news sentiment score: +1 point
- ADX < 20 (ranging market): +1 point (regime confirmation)

**COMPOSITION REQUIREMENT: Of the 3+ signals required to enter, at least 1 point must come from the oversold/dislocation group: {RSI<32, RSI<40, BelowBB, BelowVWAP>1%}. A score of 3+ composed entirely of sentiment + ADX + MA-deviation proxies does NOT qualify for entry. Log as SKIP with reason: 'No classical oversold signal in qualifying score.'**

title: Add minimum R:R filter of 1.5:1 as a hard pre-entry gate — block entry when reward < 1.5x risk regardless of signal score
analysis: Three SOL/USDT sessions on May 5 (05:40, 06:11, 06:40/07:10) showed computed R:R of 0.32, 0.49, and 0.32 respectively — all with take_profit at MA20 only $0.45-$0.85 above entry while stop was $1.41 below. The bot correctly skipped these via qualitative reasoning ('R:R of 0.32:1 — UNACCEPTABLE') but no rule encoded this rejection. The May 7 15:33 XRP session showed R:R of 1.17:1 — below 1.5 — and was skipped for the same reason. May 7 16:03 XRP showed R:R 0.91:1 — explicitly noted as below acceptable. Two META sessions that were SUBMITTED (May 6 14:37: R:R 3.7, May 7 12:45: R:R 3.33) both had qualifying R:R. The two REJECTED META entries (May 6 10:03: R:R 4.88, May 6 10:33: R:R 3.7) were rejected for position size errors, not R:R. Pattern: every attempted entry had R:R > 3.3. Every R:R < 1.5 was skipped qualitatively. The rule should formalize what the bot already consistently does.
rationale: When price is near the MA20 (the take-profit target), the distance to profit is small while the 0.5x ATR stop remains fixed. This creates naturally poor R:R during mid-band entries — exactly the conditions where the bot is most tempted to enter (mild dislocation + ADX/regime signals). A 1.5:1 minimum gate mechanically blocks entries where the mean is too close to current price, which also serves as a secondary directional gate (if take_profit is barely above entry, price is near the mean and shouldn't be a mean reversion entry).

insight.why_now: The SOL/USDT sessions on May 5 explicitly computed R:R of 0.32 in the journal entry and the bot correctly identified this as disqualifying — but this required qualitative override of a signal score that technically met the 2-point medium_safety threshold. The pattern repeated 5+ times across different symbols. The R:R check is currently an implicit qualitative rule; encoding it as a hard gate before order submission prevents any edge case where a high-signal-score trade with poor geometry slips through.
insight.purpose: Before any BUY order is submitted, compute: R:R = (take_profit - entry_price) / (entry_price - stop_loss). If R:R < 1.5, action = SKIP with reason 'Minimum R:R 1.5:1 not met (computed: X.XX:1)'. This gate operates after signal scoring and after the directional gate, as the final pre-entry check.
insight.expected_effect: Eliminates all observed sub-1.5 R:R entries from consideration without qualitative override. Has no effect on legitimate setups observed (all submitted/attempted entries had R:R > 3.3). Prevents the scenario where a late-session entry has price very near the MA20 target with a full ATR-based stop — the most common source of poor-geometry setups in this journal.
insight.risks: In fast-moving markets where price gaps below the lower BB (creating large MA20 distance), R:R will naturally be high. The 1.5x floor is conservative and should not filter valid setups. Minor risk: if MA20 is used as the sole take-profit and ATR expands significantly (high volatility), the stop distance increases and R:R could fall below 1.5 even on seemingly good setups — in this case, the correct response is to widen the take-profit target (e.g., use 25-day MA or a fixed ATR multiple) rather than lower the R:R threshold.

current_rule:
## EXIT RULES
- Take profit target: 20-day moving average (the mean)
- Partial exit: Take 50% off at the mean, trail remainder with 0.25x ATR stop
- Hard stop: 0.5x ATR below entry price
- Time stop: Exit any position open longer than 3 days regardless of P&L
proposed_rule:
## EXIT RULES
- Take profit target: 20-day moving average (the mean)
- Partial exit: Take 50% off at the mean, trail remainder with 0.25x ATR stop
- Hard stop: 0.5x ATR below entry price
- Time stop: Exit any position open longer than 3 days regardless of P&L

**MINIMUM R:R GATE (evaluated before order submission): Compute R:R = (take_profit - entry) / (entry - stop_loss). If R:R < 1.5, do NOT enter. Log as SKIP with reason 'R:R below 1.5 minimum (computed: X.XX:1)'. This gate cannot be overridden by signal score.**
